own_review_points: ParquetWriter chunk sizing uses min(batchSize, maxRowGroupRowCount - rows); since rows is only incremented after the inner loop, the term stays positive and bounded — fine but subtle
Default maxRowGroupRowCount=Integer.MAX_VALUE means MAX_VALUE - rows could be large but never negative within a single page write
MIN_ROW_GROUP_ROW_COUNT (=MIN_PAGE_VALUE_COUNT=1000) in connectors vs checkArgument>=1 in ParquetWriterOptions — inconsistent floors but intentional
Human design question: is a row-count limit the right tool vs dictionary-compression optimizer or page indexes for the underlying goal
Tests are well-constructed (default-no-flush, flush-at-limit, earlier-limit-wins) covering the feature
